Context: Cloudflare announced plans to hire 1,111 interns in 2026, a massive increase from its former 60-person internship program. The expansion is part of the company's push to build AI infrastructure and develop AI-native talent pipelines.
Growing client demand for AI deployment; reaching a total of 77,000 experts
Quadrupling its pipeline of entry-level talent in the US with a plan to hire up to 2,000 early-career talent by the end of the year.

Job Roles Created: Software developers, HR (AI oversight), customer-facing roles — redefined as AI-management positions
Context: Previously froze ~7,800 back-office roles due to AI (May 2023). Now tripling U.S. entry-level hiring for 2026, but for fundamentally different roles. New hires manage chatbots, oversee AI outputs, and intervene when AI fails — not traditional back-office work. CHRO Nickle LaMoreaux: 'The entry-level jobs from two to three years ago? AI can do most of them.' CEO Krishna (Oct 2025): 'We are probably going to hire more people out of college over the next 12 months than we have in the past few years.'
CEO and cofounder, Danny Jenkins, said company has experienced a spike in growth as it responds to a new age of threats in the AI era. Plans to nearly double its headcount in the next 18 months
